notes: Second-pass validation verifying fixes from prior reviews (batch5 for tasks 12-14, batch6 for tasks 15-16). Confirmed fixes are marked. Remaining and new issues are listed below.

findings.issue: Task 13 line 164 says 'verify Task 7 added `optional string default_model_id = 2`, `optional string last_used_model_id = 3`, `optional bool settings_keys_migrated = 4`'. Task 7 is Navigation + HomeScreen — it does not touch `app_settings.proto`. These three proto fields are added by Task 2 (Settings + core fixes), as stated explicitly in the tech-spec Task 2 description and 'Files to modify' list ('core-settings/src/main/proto/app_settings.proto'). Sending the auditor to verify a Task-7 contribution that Task 7 never makes will produce a false FAIL finding: the auditor expects Task 7 to have added those fields but Task 7 has no such scope.
findings.fix: Change line 164 from 'verify Task 7 added' to 'verify Task 2 added' — matching the actual owner of the proto change.

findings.issue: Task 13 lines 122 and 153 reference `registry.initialize(modelId)` (parameter named `modelId`). The actual `ModelRegistry.initialize` signature is `suspend fun initialize(modelName: String)` (verified in core-runtime/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/core/registry/ModelRegistry.kt line 60). Task 3 adds `activeModelName: StateFlow<String?>` but does NOT rename the `initialize` parameter. No task in the tech-spec claims ownership of renaming `initialize(modelName)` to `initialize(modelId)`. The audit checklist in focus area 6 (line 153) says auditors must verify 'WarmupCoordinator passes them to `registry.initialize(modelId)` — not `registry.initialize(modelName)`' — but both calls are identical at the call site (the parameter name is irrelevant in Kotlin positional calls); the audit intent is to verify the VALUE passed is the stable HF id, not the display name. The wording 'not `registry.initialize(modelName)`' implies the function signature itself should change, which it does not, creating false-alarm risk in the audit.
findings.fix: Rewrite line 153 to: 'WarmupCoordinator passes `AppSettings.default_model_id` / `last_used_model_id` (stable HF ids) to `registry.initialize()` — not the `Model.name` display string'. This makes the audit intent clear (value check, not parameter rename check) and aligns with the actual unchanged interface signature.

findings.issue: Task 13 post-completion checklist (line 184) only says 'Write audit report entry in decisions.md'. There is no explicit output file path for the full structured audit report — unlike Task 14 which specifies `work/phase-3-history/logs/working/task-14/security-audit-report.md`. The audit format section (line 176) says 'write findings as a structured document' and 'the report is the primary deliverable' but gives no filesystem path. An agent following the post-completion checklist will only write a summary to decisions.md and have no canonical location for the full findings.
findings.fix: Add an explicit output path such as `work/phase-3-history/logs/working/task-13/code-audit-report.md` in the post-completion checklist and in the audit report format section, analogous to Task 14's convention.

findings.issue: Task 14 context section lines 72-74 still use relative paths starting with `../../.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/` for project-knowledge files. From the task file location (`work/phase-3-history/tasks/14.md`), `../../` resolves to the `work/` directory, not the repository root — making these paths incorrect as filesystem relative references. Task 13 context section (lines 73-75) uses absolute paths (`C:/AI-WORK/PhoneWrap/.claude/...`) correctly.
findings.fix: Replace the relative paths on lines 72-74 of task 14 with absolute paths: `C:/AI-WORK/PhoneWrap/.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/project.md`, `C:/AI-WORK/PhoneWrap/.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/architecture.md`, `C:/AI-WORK/PhoneWrap/.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/patterns.md`.

findings.issue: Task 15 Details section (lines 165-213) still carries the prescriptive 'Audit scope — what each test file should cover' heading with itemized bullet lists specifying exact test cases per file. While the heading was slightly updated ('Verify the file covers...'), the structure remains test-specification rather than audit-guidance. For example: `SettingsMigrationHelperTest`: '- Happy path: all `per_model_overrides` keys rekeyed from `Model.name` to `Model.modelId` in single `updateData` call / - Orphan key (no matching allowlist entry) dropped...' — these read as TDD instructions, not audit gap criteria. An audit task should frame these as 'check whether the following branches are covered' not enumerate them as required cases to write.
findings.fix: Rephrase each bullet list prefix from imperative ('Happy path: staging files written → ...') to audit-interrogative ('Is the happy path (staging write → Room @Transaction → rename) covered as a separate test case?'). The content is correct; the framing crosses the hints-vs-implementation boundary.

findings.issue: Task 16 Verification Steps code block line 121 still contains the comment '# Step 8: ErrorLog whitelist — expect 7 lines (6 Phase-3 components + settings-io)'. The grep in that block matches 7 component strings via OR pattern. The comment is misleading: each component string may appear more than once in ErrorLog.kt (e.g., in a constant definition, a comment, and a test). The '7 lines' expectation will produce false failures if any component string appears on multiple lines — the real check should be against the ALLOWED_COMPONENTS set definition, not raw grep line count.
findings.fix: Remove or correct the '— expect 7 lines' comment. Either replace with per-component individual greps targeting the ALLOWED_COMPONENTS set specifically, or add a note: 'result may be more than 7 lines if strings appear in comments — verify each is present, not the exact count'.

confirmed_fixes.issue: ModelRegistryActiveModelTest.kt path was src/main/kotlin (wrong) — now correctly points to src/test/kotlin (line 94)
activeModelName framed as pre-existing — now says 'verify Task 3 added val activeModelName: StateFlow<String?>' (line 165)
Model.modelId framed as pre-existing — now says 'verify Task 2 added val modelId: String field' (line 166)
ModelEntry.isDownloaded context — partially addressed with '(field added by Task 2)' note at line 124; field name still used in focus area
depends_on was [12] only — now correctly includes [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 12]
aapt command was missing APK path — now correctly specifies app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk (line 130)
